lede: A look at January 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Walker Scobell and C. J. Cron, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Alaska Airlines Flight 1282
events.desc: Alaska Airlines Flight 1282 makes an emergency landing at Portland International Airport after a door plug blows off the Boeing 737 MAX 9 operating the flight. There are no fatalities, but the accident prompts the 737 MAX to be grounded and renews scrutiny on Boeing's manufacturing and design issues.

events.title: Somali Civil War
events.desc: Somali Civil War: The United States Embassy to Somalia in Mogadishu is evacuated by helicopter airlift days after the outbreak of violence in Mogadishu.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: Gunmen shoot dead ten Protestant civilians after stopping their minibus at Kingsmill in County Armagh, Northern Ireland, UK, allegedly as retaliation for a string of attacks on Catholic civilians in the area by Loyalists, particularly the killing of six Catholics the night before.

events.title: 1970 Tonghai earthquake
events.desc: The 7.1 Mw Tonghai earthquake shakes Tonghai County, Yunnan province, China, with a maximum Mercalli intensity of X (Extreme). Between 10,000 and 15,000 people are known to have been killed and about another 26,000 are injured.

events.title: United States Congress
events.desc: In a speech given to the United States Congress, United States President Dwight D. Eisenhower announces the establishment of what will later be called the Eisenhower Doctrine.

events.title: Golden Gate Bridge
events.desc: Construction of the Golden Gate Bridge begins in San Francisco Bay.

events.title: Nellie Tayloe Ross
events.desc: Nellie Tayloe Ross of Wyoming becomes the first female governor in the United States.

events.title: German Workers' Party
events.desc: The German Workers' Party, which would become the Nazi Party, is founded in Munich.

events.title: Russian Social Democratic Labour Party
events.desc: The sixth All-Russian Conference of the Russian Social Democratic Labour Party (Prague Party Conference) opens. In the course of the conference, Vladimir Lenin and his supporters break from the rest of the party to form the Bolshevik movement.

events.title: Louis XV
events.desc: Louis XV of France survives an assassination attempt by Robert-François Damiens, who becomes the last person to be executed in France by drawing and quartering (the traditional form of capital punishment used for regicides).

births.name: Joé Juneau
births.line: Canadian ice hockey player and engineer

births.name: John Manley
births.line: Canadian lawyer and politician, 8th Deputy Prime Minister of Canada

deaths.text: Qazi Hussain Ahmad, Pakistani scholar and politician
Victor Hope, 2nd Marquess of Linlithgow, Scottish colonel and politician, 46th Governor-General of India

observances.text: Christian Feast day: Charles of Mount Argus
Harbin International Ice and Snow Sculpture Festival (Harbin, China)
